activity.title: Devs, Are Your AI Agents Playing Solo? Let's Get Them Jamming Together with A2A! 🤖💬🤝 Alright developers, picture this: you're using some seriously cool AI agents. One writes (and posts 👀) your LinkedIn posts like a pro, another effortlessly books movie tickets for you, and a third seamlessly manages your calendar and scheduling. Awesome! But... getting them to actually work together across different systems or frameworks? Often feels like wrestling APIs from different galaxies, right? 😅 Enter the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol! Spearheaded by Google and a whole crew of tech partners, A2A is an open protocol aiming to fix this headache. Think of A2A as the standardized handshake + chat protocol for AI agents. It's designed so agents built by anyone, on any framework, can discover each other, communicate securely, and actually collaborate on tasks. "How do they even FIND each other?" 🤔 Good question! A2A uses an "Agent Card" - basically a standardized agent.json file just like OpenID (at /.well-known/agent.json), kinda like a business card and service menu combined, but for AI agents. It tells other agents: "Here's who I am, here are my skills (my 'API endpoints' or capabilities), and here's how to securely talk to me!" Pretty neat for discovery, right? Okay, so how does the "chat" part work? Under the hood, it's built on familiar ground: HTTP, JSON-RPC. One agent (the "Client Agent") sends a task request to another (the "Remote Agent"). They exchange standardized Messages containing Parts (think text, files, structured JSON data). For those longer tasks (deep analysis, anyone?), it supports streaming via Server-Sent Events (SSE) or even push notifications, so clients don't have to just sit and wait. ⏳➡️✅ "Is this just another name for Anthropic's MCP?" Nope! They're buddies, not rivals. 🤝 • MCP (Model Context Protocol): Think of it as giving one agent better tools and context for its own brain. 🧠💡 • A2A: Think of it as defining the conversation rules so multiple agents can coordinate as a team. 🗣️👥 They solve different problems and can definitely work together! Why should YOU, the developer, care? A2A offers a standard way for diverse AI agents to communicate, reducing integration headaches and unlocking exciting possibilities for complex, collaborative AI workflows. Ready to see how your agents can team up? Explore the A2A project! Read the article: https://lnkd.in/g2b_E5_T And check it out on GitHub: https://lnkd.in/gvzfi6RH . What cool multi-agent workflows pop into your head? Drop your ideas below! 👇 #AI #AgentInteroperability #A2A #SoftwareDevelopment #DevCommunity #OpenSource #API #GoogleAI #LLMs #FutureOfCode #Integration ...more

11 AM to 4 AM. Daily. For 10 Days Straight. That’s what it took to turn an idea into something real. Not a class project. Not a hackathon. Just a bunch of builders obsessed with solving a problem every student faces — where do I find the right internship? We sketched flows, broke code, fixed bugs, redesigned screens at 2 AM — then did it all over again the next day. Every single person brought their madness, and somehow, it clicked. ✨ InternWare proudly presents our ultimate flagship event, InternHunt 8.0: The Internship Fair, is set for May 03, 2025! To make this event more than just a notice board of roles, we built a full-fledged platform from scratch — a clean, focused space where students can explore curated internship opportunities and apply directly with zero hassle. Whether you're a fresher or someone looking to level up, this is where your internship journey kicks off. Built by: Myself, LAKSHAY MAHAJAN and Priyanshu Negi With immense guidance and support from our mentors: Ashish Jangra and Ashutosh Kumar — thank you for pushing us to build better. 🙌 And if you haven't registered yet, 👀 Check it out now: https://lnkd.in/gvM95PcB Let the hunt begin. Because this summer, opportunities won’t knock — they’ll hunt you down. 🎯 #InternHunt8 #Internware #IITM #InternshipFair #Internships #StudentOpportunities #TechForStudents #BuiltDifferent #OpportunityUnlocked ...more

🚀 AI just got a serious upgrade! 🎨🤖 By now, your feed is probably drowning in "OMG, look at my Ghibli-style AI masterpiece!" posts. But let’s cut through the noise—because as cool as that is, the real innovation is happening under the hood. 🔥 What’s Under the Hood? AI-generated images aren’t just diffusion-based anymore. The latest models use a hybrid approach: 🖼 Auto-regressive transformers → Instead of randomly guessing pixels, the AI generates structured image tokens sequentially (like an artist who actually plans their painting). 🌌 Latent space representations → The model doesn’t work directly with pixels; it compresses images into an efficient form, which is why the results are sharper and faster. 🎨 Diffusion models → They still play a role in refining textures and adding the final touch of realism (or AI weirdness, depending on the prompt). This combo makes AI better at understanding text, avoiding bizarre artifacts, and generating high-quality details—which means fewer nightmare-fuel images and more "Wow, that actually looks legit." 🛠 Why Should Devs Care? 🔹 Faster Prototyping – No more waiting on designers; just describe your vision and let AI suffer instead. 🔹 Dynamic Asset Generation – Game devs, marketers, and startups can now generate visuals without paying for overpriced stock photos. 🔹 Style Control & Refinement – With the right prompt, you can switch from "Hyper-realistic Sci-Fi" to "I let a toddler use MS Paint." 💡 How to Use It Like a Pro? 1️⃣ Be descriptive but realistic ("A futuristic cyberpunk city with rain reflections and neon lights," not "Make me the next Pixar film."). 2️⃣ Use weighted prompts if supported ("More contrast, less blur, sharper edges, and no cursed fingers, please."). 3️⃣ Experiment! AI is basically rolling the dice in latent space—so expect a mix of masterpieces and digital horrors. 💭 Where do you see AI-generated images actually being useful? Or are we just creating an army of aesthetic but pointless wallpapers? At this rate, AI might replace designers… or just give them more existential crises. Either way, fun times ahead. 🤷‍♂️ Coders thought their jobs were in danger. Now it’s the designers’ turn. 👀 Let’s discuss! 👇🔥 #AI #MachineLearning #GenerativeAI #ImageGeneration #DevLife ...more

This intensive bootcamp will equip me with the skills to build impactful dApps, analyze smart contracts, and navigate the ever-evolving Solana ecosystem. I am really looking forward to an incredible journey of learning new things with an amazing instructor from the Rise In Team. A huge thank you to the Rise In team for making this possible! I'm ready to dive in headfirst and connect with fellow learners - any Rise In Bootcampers out there? Let's share tips and support each other on this exciting journey! Once again Thankyou Rise In Team for this great opportunity! ❤️ #riseinbootcamp #solana #blockchain #learningjourney ...more

“Great moments are born from great opportunities.” – Herb Brooks InternWare - The Internship Cell of IITM proudly presents glimpses of the Fourth Edition of उMUNg - Minds In Motion, held on February 23-24, 2026. From powerful debates and thought-provoking discussions to unforgettable connections and inspiring leadership, every moment reflected passion, diplomacy, and determination. उMUNg wasn’t just an event, it was an experience in motion. Relive the energy. Reignite the spirit!

The Institute of Information Technology & Management (IITM), affiliated to Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University, successfully organized UMUNG 4.0, the fourth edition of its Annual Model United Nations, under the aegis of InternWare – Internship Cell, on 23–24 February 2026. The two-day conference witnessed enthusiastic participation from delegates across various cities, fostering a vibrant platform for intellectual exchange and diplomatic engagement. The event featured dynamic committee sessions, insightful deliberations, and a series of healthy debates on diverse global agendas. Beyond the formal proceedings, UMUNG 4.0 offered extensive opportunities for networking, collaboration, and meaningful interactions. From engaging social sessions to enjoyable culinary experiences, the conference seamlessly blended learning, leadership, and celebration—making it a memorable and enriching experience for all participants.

I used to wait. Wait until I have something big to say. A win worth announcing. An achievement that sounded impressive enough. So I stayed silent on most days. On the days I struggled. On the days I learned something small. On the days I showed up but didn’t “win.” I thought those moments didn’t deserve a post. But looking back, Those were the moments shaping me the most. The truth is, growth rarely comes with applause. It comes quietly, in confusion, in consistency, in showing up anyway. And maybe those stories matter more than the highlight reel. Because success isn’t built on big announcements. It’s built on all the days we thought weren’t worth sharing.
